claims.claim: Tnufa (Ideation) grants up to NIS 200,000 over 12 months at 80% of an approved budget capped at NIS 250,000
Israeli company registration fee 2026 is NIS 2,614 one-time
Companies Registrar annual fee is NIS 1,338 if paid by 31 March 2026, NIS 1,777 from 1 April 2026; first-year exemption applies
Standard Israeli VAT rose to 18% on 1 January 2025 (from 17%)
Osek Patur (exempt-dealer) annual turnover ceiling is NIS 122,833 for 2026
Preferred Technological Enterprise tax rate is 12% (7.5% in Area A) on qualifying IP income; SPTE rate is 6%
PTE eligibility requires R&D >= 7% of revenue for prior 3 years OR R&D > NIS 75M per year, plus 20% revenue from Israeli-developed IP
Israeli standard corporate tax rate remains 23% in 2026
From 2026 closely-held companies (5 or fewer shareholders) face allocation of undistributed profits to shareholders taxed at marginal rates up to 50%
OECD Pillar Two QDMTT applies to multinational groups (global revenue >= EUR 750M) for tax years starting after 31 December 2025 with 15% effective tax floor
Section 102 capital-gains-track trustee must hold options and exercised shares at least 24 months from the END of the tax year of grant (not from grant date)
Section 102 capital-gains-track rate is 25% flat (30% for 10%+ controlling shareholders) with a 3% surtax above the high-income threshold
BIRD General Program max grant is USD 1.5M at up to 50% of project R&D costs; BIRD HLS max is USD 1M
Yozma 2.0 is a fund-of-funds with 0.3:1 government:institutional ratio; first round combined USD 700M started Q3 2024
January 2024 stimulus package allocated roughly USD 1B for Israeli VC funds 2024-2026, plus NIS 940M additional IIA budget on top of NIS 1.5B base
Approximately 45% of newly-formed Israeli tech startups incorporated abroad in 2025, up from about 20% in 2022
Typical YC post-money SAFE caps 2026: pre-seed USD 3-8M, seed USD 8-20M; discount 15-25% (20% common)
Israel Innovation Authority 2026 program list includes Tnufa, Startup Fund, R&D Fund, Technological Venture Incubators, Magnet/Magneton/Nofar/Kamin, BIRD, Horizon Europe
IIA-funded IP transfer abroad requires authority approval and a transfer fee of up to 6x the grant amount
